name: Gereja Katolik Paroki Santo Petrus Denpasar
description: Paroki Monang Maning berawal dari Sektor Paulus Roncali di Paroki St. Yoseph Denpasar sejak tahun 1946. Kemudian pada tahun 1980 umat di wilayah Perumnas Monang Maning membentuk sektor sendiri, yaitu Sektor St. Petrus, terpisah dari Sektor Paulus Roncali. Pendirian Gereja St. Petrus dimulai sejak 1 Juni 1987 di lokasinya saat ini, di Jalan Gunung Batok, dan diberkati pada 20 Desember 1987 oleh Uskup Denpasar Mgr. Vitalis Djebarus, SVD. Pada 15 Juni 1995 statusnya ditingkatkan menjadi Quasi Paroki St. Petrus Monang Maning. Seiring dengan perkembangan umat, pada tahun 1999 dilakukan pembongkaran gereja (gedung) untuk dibangun gereja baru yang lebih representatif.

name: Threads of Life Natural Dye Studio
description: Step into the world of plant-based color traditions at Threads of Life Dye Studio, 15 minutes north of central Ubud. Set in a peaceful natural dye garden with plants like Indigo and Morinda, our workshops explore Indonesia's natural dyes and their cultural significance. In our half-day classes, create your own textile using batik or resist-dye methods. For deeper engagement, our 2-Day Workshop explores four millennia of dye heritage through surface design and piece dyeing with indigo, Ceriops brown, and mud-dye black. Rooted in field research since 1998, the course offers insight into both tradition and technique, and can lead to further personal practice. Each class supports heritage, ecology, and women's livelihoods across Indonesia.

name: PT. Inspiral
description:
Inspiral is an international award-winning architecture studio based in Bali, specializing in biocentric, nature-driven design.

The practice creates sustainable, site-responsive architecture that strengthens the relationship between the built environment and natural systems, integrating passive strategies, innovative construction techniques, and environmentally conscious materials.

Working across hospitality, residential, and commercial sectors, Inspiral delivers projects ranging from eco-resorts and retreats to private villas, with an international outlook rooted in tropical design.

The studio's work has been widely recognized, including the UNESCO Prix Versailles for sustainability, and featured in international publications.

GII HIT Kuta memulai pelayanan di Bali pada tahun 2006 dan hadir untuk mentransformasi keluarga supaya bisa mentransformasi dunia.

description:
Mangrove forests are unique ecosystems consisting of shrubs or mangrove trees that grow in the intertidal zone (tidal) of coastal areas, river estuaries, or muddy areas with brackish water and low oxygen levels. They play a vital role in preventing erosion, protecting coastlines, providing habitat for diverse fauna, and absorbing blue carbon. Their distinctive characteristics include a dense root system (prop roots) that retains sediment and tidal seawater, as well as the plants' adaptation to high salinity.

Our restaurant is located in Legian and welcomes you with its cozy and natural atmosphere. Our menu features a diverse range of Balinese dishes, skillfully prepared by our talented chefs using only the freshest local ingredients available in the market.

We take pride in offering excellent customer service with our friendly and attentive staff, who will go above and beyond to make your dining experience unforgettable.

Whether you're seeking a romantic dinner for two, a family gathering, or a night out with friends, our restaurant is the perfect choice for you.
We also offer special services for your special events and occasions to make it an unforgettable experience for all your guests.

description: Bhuana Agung Jatiluwih located in the Unesco Jatiluwih Tabanan Bali Area. The area is so famous in Bali for beautiful rice fields with the background of Batukaru mountain. In Bhuana Agung you will be spoiled with a view of the rice fields managed by the traditional Balinese irrigation system known as subak. Almost every area in Bali has rundown rice fields. However, Jatiluwih is a stretch of rice fields reaching more than four hundred hectares. Jatiluwih is located in Penebel District, Tabanan Regency, Bali. Welcome to Banjar Segara, where tradition meets tranquility. Hidden away in a quiet village near the coast, this villa offers a private retreat designed with timeless Balinese Javanese fushion charm -- teakwood walls, handcrafted furniture, and an open-air bathtub under the sky.

Wake up to the scent of brewed coffee. Bathe outdoors as the sun rises. Spend the evening on your private balcony, watching the sky fade over rice fields. We don't offer five-star opulence -- we offer something rarer: sincerity, silence, and soul.
